title: Using R for the first time
text: get started R, need install R RStudio, user-friendly interface R. First, download R Comprehensive R Archive Network (CRAN), follow installation instructions operating system. installing R, download RStudio follow prompts complete installation. plenty online resources available guide step. R: https://cloud.r-project.org/ RStudio: https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/ Useful starting point RStudio Education: Finding Way R

what: Loading a Package and Basic Introduction to RStudio
title: Using R for the first time
text: first open RStudio, ’ll see layout multiple panes. main areas Console (can type execute R commands), Source Editor (write edit R scripts), Environment/History pane, Files/Plots/Packages/Help pane.

previous_headings: Loading a Package and Basic Introduction to RStudio
what: Loading a Package for the First Time
title: Using R for the first time
text: load package R first time, follow two steps: Installing package: Open Console RStudio type: Loading package: installation, load package R session using library() function:

previous_headings: Loading a Package and Basic Introduction to RStudio
title: Using R for the first time
text: Creating new R script: Click File > New File > R Script. Running code: Place cursor line code press Ctrl+Enter (Cmd+Enter Mac). Viewing installed packages: bottom-right pane, click “Packages” tab. Getting help: Type ?function_name Console. example: Viewing plots: Created plots appear “Plots” tab bottom-right pane. Managing files: Use “Files” tab bottom-right pane navigate project directory.

title: ConSciR
text: ConSciR R package specifically designed assist conservators scientists providing toolkit performing essential calculations streamlining common tasks conservation.

title: ConSciR
ConSciR
text: ensure optimal functionality ConSciR functions, crucial maintain data tidy consistently formatted structure. allows code easily locate process variables. Please note specific naming conventions columns essential package work correctly. following formats recommended:

title: ConSciR
ConSciR
text: Key Points: Column Names: Try use exact column names shown (Site, Sensor, Date, Temp, RH, Lux, UV). names case-sensitive. Tools tidy files developed. Date Format: Date column ideally datetime format (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS). Check behaviour dates format hasn’t used. Numeric Data: Ensure Temp, RH, Lux, UV columns contain numeric data. Categorical Data: Site Sensor categorical variables character strings. Tidy Structure: row represents single observation, column represents single variable. Missing Columns: Include relevant columns, even data points missing (leave blank use NA missing values).

title: ConSciR
text: Calculations: dew point, absolute humidity, water vapour pressure, mixing ratio, enthalpy Graphs: TRH plots, psychometric chart Summaries: percentiles Analysis: Tidying data: Examples use:
code: # Load packages library(ConSciR) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) # Add calculated values using mutate head(mydata) |>   mutate(     AbsHum = calcAH(Temp, RH),      DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH),     MixingRatio = calcMR(Temp, RH),     Enthalpy = calcEnthalpy(Temp, RH),      IPI = calcIPI(Temp, RH),      PI = calcPI(Temp, RH)   ) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH AbsHum DewPoint MixingRatio #>   <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8   7.07     6.41        5.96 #> 2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7   7.05     6.37        5.94 #> 3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6   7.03     6.33        5.93 #> 4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6   6.99     6.24        5.89 #> 5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5   6.97     6.20        5.87 #> 6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2   6.91     6.08        5.83 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: Enthalpy <dbl>, IPI <dbl>, PI <dbl> # Visualise and explore data mydata |>   mutate(     AbsHum = calcAH(Temp, RH),      DewPoint = calcDP(Temp, RH)   ) |>   ggplot() +    geom_line(aes(Date, Temp), col = "red") +    geom_line(aes(Date, RH), col = "blue") +   geom_line(aes(Date, AbsHum), col = "green") +   geom_line(aes(Date, DewPoint), col = "purple") +   lims(y = c(0, 100)) +    labs(x = "Date", y = "Value", title = "Sensor",         subtitle = "Temperature (red), relative humidity (blue), absolute humidity (green), dew point (purple)", caption = "ConSciR") +   theme_bw()

title: Tools for Conservation Science
text: ConSciR package documentation available : ConSciR Package Documentation ConSciR Github page: ConSciR Github

title: Tools for Conservation Science
text: ConSciR R package specifically designed assist conservators scientists providing toolkit performing essential calculations streamlining common tasks conservation. ConSciR designed : Conservators working museums, galleries, heritage sites Conservation scientists researchers Cultural heritage professionals involved preventive conservation Students educators conservation heritage science programs

title: Tools for Conservation Science
text: ConSciR package built principle making complex calculations accessible reproducible cultural heritage community. providing standardised set tools, aims : Enhance accuracy consistency conservation-related calculations Facilitate collaboration knowledge sharing within field Promote adoption data-driven approaches conservation practice

title: Tools for Conservation Science
text: ConSciR dynamic package evolves field conservation science : Update functions reflect latest research findings Incorporate feedback user community improve functionality Add new tools features address emerging needs field encourage users contribute expertise suggestions help shape future development ConSciR, ensuring remains valuable resource cultural heritage community.

what: Calculate enthalpy from temperature (C) and relative humidity (%) — calcEnthalpy
title: Calculate enthalpy from temperature (C) and relative humidity (%) — calcEnthalpy
text: Function calculate enthalpy temperature Celsius relative humidity % (0-100). using dataframe, columns ideally named "Temp" "RH". Example: ambient temperature 20B0C relative humidity 50 Pw = Pws(20B0C) B7 50/100 = 11.69 hPa X = 621.9907 B7 11.69/(1013-11.69) = 7.26 g/kg h = 20 B7 (1.01 + 0.00189 B7 7.26) + 2.5 B7 7.26 = 38.62 kJ/kg

title: Psychrometric chart from temperature and humidity — graph_psychrometric
code: data <- data.frame(Temp = c(20, 25, 30), RH = c(50, 60, 70)) graph_psychrometric(data, Temp, RH, LowT = 15, HighT = 25, LowRH = 40, HighRH = 60) #> Warning: All aesthetics have length 1, but the data has 3 rows. #> ℹ Please consider using `annotate()` or provide this layer with data containing #>   a single row. #> Warning: All aesthetics have length 1, but the data has 3 rows. #> ℹ Please consider using `annotate()` or provide this layer with data containing #>   a single row. #> Warning: Removed 12 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`). #> Warning: Removed 27 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`). #> Warning: Removed 45 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`). #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).   graph_psychrometric(head(mydata), Temp, RH, LowT = 15, HighT = 25, LowRH = 40, HighRH = 60) #> Warning: All aesthetics have length 1, but the data has 6 rows. #> ℹ Please consider using `annotate()` or provide this layer with data containing #>   a single row. #> Warning: All aesthetics have length 1, but the data has 6 rows. #> ℹ Please consider using `annotate()` or provide this layer with data containing #>   a single row. #> Warning: Removed 12 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`). #> Warning: Removed 27 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`). #> Warning: Removed 45 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`).
